status.state_description: Waiting for human approval of the plan
status.product_description: CRM platform with contact management, sales pipeline, email automation, lead scoring, reporting dashboards, and Salesforce migration tools.

plan.description: Analyze CRM market landscape: Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive positioning. Identify addressable segments, pricing benchmarks, migration pain points, and differentiation opportunities for crm_platform.
acceptance_criteria: Competitor feature matrix covering all 6 product areas is complete
Pricing benchmarks documented for at least 5 competitors
Top 3 migration pain points from Salesforce identified with evidence
TAM/SAM/SOM estimates included with sources

plan.description: Define business requirements, user personas (sales rep, sales manager, RevOps, admin), core user stories for all 6 feature areas, and process flows for lead-to-close lifecycle.
acceptance_criteria: At least 5 user stories per persona with acceptance criteria
Lead-to-close process flow diagram covers all pipeline stages
Salesforce migration user journey documented end-to-end
ROI analysis quantifies time-savings vs. Salesforce per persona
Pricing tiers defined
Churn risk factors identified

plan.description: Produce product requirements document (PRD), phased roadmap (MVP → v1 → v2), feature prioritization matrix (RICE scoring), and success metrics (activation rate, pipeline velocity, churn).
acceptance_criteria: PRD covers all 6 feature areas with scope boundaries per phase
RICE scores assigned to every feature in backlog
MVP scope is achievable in ≤12 weeks by a 3-person team
Success metrics defined with baseline, target, and measurement method

plan.description: Build financial model: infrastructure cost projections, SaaS pricing tiers (Starter/Growth/Enterprise), unit economics (CAC, LTV, payback period), and 3-year P&L forecast.
acceptance_criteria: Unit economics model includes CAC, LTV, LTV:CAC ratio, payback period
Infrastructure cost modeled at 100, 1K, 10K seat cohorts
Three pricing tiers defined with feature gates and per-seat prices
Break-even analysis shows month of profitability under conservative assumptions
MRR/ARR projections modeled
CAC and LTV estimated

plan.description: Draft Terms of Service, Privacy Policy (GDPR/CCPA compliant), Data Processing Agreement (DPA) template for enterprise customers, and email automation CAN-SPAM/CASL compliance review.
acceptance_criteria: Privacy Policy covers all GDPR Articles 13/14 disclosure requirements
DPA template includes SCCs for EU data transfers
Email automation compliance checklist covers unsubscribe, sender ID, and consent requirements
ToS includes data ownership, SLA definitions, and termination clauses
Subscription terms cover cancellation and refunds
Data processing agreement drafted

plan.description: Produce threat model (STRIDE) for CRM platform covering contact data, email credentials, pipeline data, and Salesforce OAuth tokens. Define security architecture: encryption at rest/transit, RBAC, audit logging.
acceptance_criteria: STRIDE analysis covers all 6 major components with mitigations
RBAC matrix defined for all 4 personas with least-privilege principle
Salesforce OAuth token storage design reviewed and approved
Audit log schema captures actor, action, resource, timestamp for all mutations

plan.description: User research synthesis (jobs-to-be-done), information architecture, wireframes for all major flows: contact list, pipeline kanban, email campaign builder, lead score card, dashboard, and Salesforce import wizard.
acceptance_criteria: Wireframes cover all 6 product areas with annotated interaction notes
Pipeline kanban supports drag-and-drop stage transitions
Email builder wireframe shows visual block editor (not raw HTML)
Salesforce import wizard wireframe covers field-mapping and conflict resolution steps
Accessibility audit checklist passed (color contrast, keyboard nav, ARIA roles)

plan.description: Define SOC 2 Type II control framework for crm_platform: map product features to Trust Service Criteria (Security, Availability, Confidentiality), produce evidence collection plan and control matrix.
acceptance_criteria: All CC, A, and C criteria mapped to at least one technical or operational control
Evidence collection plan lists owner, frequency, and artifact format per control
Gap analysis identifies controls not yet implemented
Audit readiness checklist produced for each Trust Service Criterion

plan.description: Design relational schema for CRM core: contacts, accounts, deals, activities, pipeline_stages, email_campaigns, email_sends, lead_scores, users, roles, audit_events. Include indexes, foreign keys, and soft-delete patterns.
acceptance_criteria: ERD covers all 14 tables with relationships and cardinalities
Row-level security policies enforce tenant isolation
All foreign keys have cascading delete or restrict rules defined
Indexes defined for all query patterns identified in user stories
Migration files runnable via Alembic from empty schema to full schema

plan.description: Design OpenAPI 3.1 spec for all CRM REST endpoints: contacts CRUD, pipeline stages, deals, activities, email campaigns, lead scoring rules, reports, Salesforce OAuth + sync, and webhook delivery.
acceptance_criteria: All endpoints documented with request/response schemas and error codes
Pagination (cursor-based) defined for all list endpoints
Salesforce OAuth flow (authorize → callback → token refresh) fully specified
Webhook delivery spec includes retry policy and signature verification
OpenAPI spec validates without errors against openapi-validator

plan.description: Set up monorepo project structure, Python backend (FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + Alembic), React frontend (Vite + TypeScript), Docker Compose for local dev (Postgres, Redis, Mailhog), pre-commit hooks, and environment config.
acceptance_criteria: `docker compose up` starts all services with no errors
Backend server starts on :8000 with `/health` returning 200
Frontend dev server starts on :3000 with no TypeScript errors
pre-commit hooks run ruff + mypy on Python, eslint + prettier on TS
.env.example documents all required environment variables

plan.description: Implement contact management API: CRUD for contacts and accounts, custom field definitions, activity timeline, duplicate detection (fuzzy match on email/phone), bulk import (CSV), and advanced search with filters.
acceptance_criteria: All CRUD endpoints return correct HTTP status codes and response shapes per OpenAPI spec
Duplicate detection flags contacts with >85% similarity score before save
CSV import handles 10K rows without timeout; errors reported per-row
Custom field definitions stored and returned with contact objects
Activity timeline returns events sorted desc by timestamp with pagination

plan.description: Implement sales pipeline API: configurable pipeline stages, deal CRUD with stage transitions, weighted forecast calculation, deal activity logging, stuck deal detection (configurable days in stage), and bulk stage move.
acceptance_criteria: Stage transition enforces valid-next-stage rules defined in pipeline config
Weighted forecast aggregates by owner, team, and close date with correct math
Stuck deal API returns deals exceeding threshold with days_in_stage field
All deal mutations write to audit_events table
Bulk stage move handles 500 deals atomically (all succeed or all fail)

plan.description: Implement email automation engine: campaign builder (sequences, triggers, delays), per-contact send scheduling via Celery, open/click tracking (pixel + redirect), unsubscribe handling, bounce/complaint processing, and sending via SMTP/SendGrid.
acceptance_criteria: Campaign sequences support branching on open/click/no-response conditions
Celery workers schedule sends within 60s of trigger firing
Open tracking pixel fires and increments open_count atomically
Unsubscribe link processing suppresses contact within 10s and never sends again
Bounce/complaint webhooks from SendGrid processed and contact suppressed
CAN-SPAM required headers (From, Subject, physical address) enforced before send

plan.description: Implement AI-powered lead scoring engine using Multi-Agent Coordinator pattern: coordinator decomposes scoring into specialist agents (demographic fit, behavioral signals, engagement score), aggregates into 0-100 composite score, and supports custom rule overrides.
acceptance_criteria: Coordinator agent decomposes scoring task to 5 specialist dimension agents
Composite score computed and stored within 5s of trigger event
Score history preserved per contact (not overwritten)
Custom rule overrides applied after base model without changing model weights
Score explanation (top 3 factors) returned via GET /contacts/{id}/lead-score
Nightly batch recompute completes for 100K contacts in under 30 minutes

plan.description: Implement reporting and analytics API: pre-built reports (pipeline by stage, email performance, lead source attribution, activity leaderboard), custom report builder (dimensions + metrics + filters), scheduled report delivery, and CSV/PDF export.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 pre-built reports return correct aggregates verified against raw DB data
Custom report builder accepts arbitrary dimension/metric/filter combinations without SQL injection risk
Reports with 1M rows export to CSV in under 60s via streaming response
PDF export renders charts (bar, line, pie) as embedded SVG
Scheduled reports delivered within 5 minutes of configured send time

plan.description: Build Salesforce migration toolchain: OAuth2 connection to Salesforce org, field mapping engine (Salesforce field → crm_platform field), delta sync (initial full + ongoing incremental via Salesforce Change Data Capture), conflict resolution, and rollback capability.
acceptance_criteria: OAuth2 flow connects to Salesforce sandbox and production orgs without exposing tokens in logs
Field mapping UI allows drag-and-drop mapping with type coercion warnings
Full import of 100K contacts completes using Bulk API 2 in under 15 minutes
Incremental CDC processes events within 5 minutes of Salesforce change
Rollback restores pre-import snapshot atomically with no partial state
All 8 Salesforce object types successfully imported with relationship integrity

plan.description: Build contact management UI: contact list with virtual scrolling, detail view with activity timeline and custom fields, quick-edit inline, bulk actions (tag, assign, export), duplicate merge UI, and CSV import wizard.
acceptance_criteria: Contact list virtualises rows and renders 10K contacts with no visible lag
Activity timeline shows all event types with correct icons and relative timestamps
Inline edit commits on blur with optimistic UI update and error rollback
Duplicate merge UI shows field-by-field comparison with winner selection
CSV import wizard shows column preview, field mapping, and per-row error report

plan.description: Build pipeline kanban UI: drag-and-drop deal cards between stages (react-dnd), deal detail panel, stage summary (count + value), forecast sidebar, stuck deal highlighting, and bulk stage move.
acceptance_criteria: Drag-and-drop stage move fires PATCH /deals/{id}/move and updates UI optimistically
Stage columns show deal count and total value in header
Forecast sidebar updates in real-time when deals are moved
Stuck deals highlighted with amber border after configurable days threshold
Board renders 200 deal cards across 8 stages with no jank on drag

plan.description: Build email campaign builder UI: visual block editor (drag-and-drop text, image, button blocks), sequence flow canvas (trigger → steps → branches), preview (desktop/mobile), A/B test setup, and send scheduler.
acceptance_criteria: Block editor produces valid HTML email output renderable in Mailhog preview
Sequence canvas renders trigger → step → branch flows with connecting edges
Desktop/mobile preview toggle shows responsive layout differences
A/B test config allows up to 4 variants with percentage split
Scheduled send shows user's local timezone and converts to UTC for API

plan.description: Build lead scoring UI: score card per contact (gauge, top factors, history chart), scoring rule configuration panel (dimension weights, custom rules), and bulk score view with filter/sort.
acceptance_criteria: Score gauge renders 0-100 with colour zones (cold/warm/hot) per configured thresholds
Top 3 score factors displayed with delta vs. previous score
Score history chart shows 90-day trend line
Rule editor validates weight totals sum to 100% before save
Bulk score table sorts and filters 10K contacts without server round-trip

plan.description: Build reporting dashboards UI: pre-built dashboard with drag-and-drop widget layout, custom report builder (dimension/metric picker, chart type selector), scheduled report config, and export controls.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard widgets draggable and resizable; layout persists to backend
Chart renderer supports bar, line, pie, funnel, and table widget types
Report builder generates preview in under 3s for datasets up to 100K rows
Export button triggers CSV streaming download with progress indicator
Scheduled report modal shows cron-like human-readable schedule preview

plan.description: Build Salesforce migration wizard UI: OAuth connect flow, object selection checklist, field mapping drag-and-drop canvas, pre-import validation report, live progress tracker, conflict resolution review, and rollback control.
acceptance_criteria: OAuth connect flow opens popup, captures token, and shows connected org name
Object selector shows record counts per Salesforce object before import
Field mapping canvas shows type-mismatch warnings in amber, incompatible types in red
Validation report downloadable as CSV before confirming import
Progress tracker polls import job status every 5s with records_processed / total
Rollback button disabled after 48h post-import with tooltip explanation

plan.description: Write backend unit and integration tests: pytest suite covering all API endpoints, business logic (lead scoring math, forecast calculation, duplicate detection), Celery tasks, and Salesforce sync engine. Target ≥85% code coverage.
acceptance_criteria: pytest suite runs to completion with 0 failures on clean checkout
Coverage report shows ≥85% line coverage across all backend modules
Salesforce sync tests use mock responses covering happy path + error cases
Lead scoring unit tests verify composite score formula with known inputs
Integration tests hit real Postgres (testcontainer) not mocks

plan.description: Write frontend component and E2E tests: Vitest unit tests for all React components, Playwright E2E covering critical user journeys (create contact, move deal, launch campaign, run report, complete Salesforce import).
acceptance_criteria: All Vitest component tests pass with ≥80% branch coverage
Playwright tests run against local docker-compose stack end-to-end
Each of 5 critical journeys passes in <30s on CI
Visual regression snapshots captured for all dashboard widgets
Accessibility tests (axe-core) run as part of Playwright suite with 0 critical violations

plan.description: Build CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions): lint → test → build → container push → staging deploy → E2E → production deploy (manual gate). Include branch protection rules, environment secrets management, and Dependabot config.
acceptance_criteria: CI pipeline completes lint + test + build in under 10 minutes
Docker images tagged with git SHA and pushed to GHCR on merge to main
Staging deploys automatically on merge; production requires manual approval
Branch protection requires 1 approval + passing CI before merge to main
Dependabot configured for Python, Node, and Docker base images

plan.description: Define production infrastructure as code (Terraform): Postgres RDS (Multi-AZ), ElastiCache Redis, ECS Fargate services (API, Celery workers, beat scheduler), ALB with WAF, S3 for exports, CloudWatch dashboards and alarms.
acceptance_criteria: `terraform plan` produces no errors on clean AWS account with required permissions
RDS Multi-AZ with automated backups (7-day retention) configured
WAF rules block OWASP Top 10 attack patterns
CloudWatch alarms defined for API p99 latency >1s, error rate >1%, Celery queue depth >1000
All secrets stored in AWS Secrets Manager, not Terraform state
Staging and production environments deployable from same Terraform with workspace var files

plan.description: Write operational runbooks: incident response (P1/P2/P3 classification), on-call rotation setup, database failover procedure, Celery queue drain, Salesforce sync re-trigger, SLA definitions, and capacity planning model.
acceptance_criteria: Incident response runbook classifies P1/P2/P3 with response time SLAs per tier
Database failover runbook tested against staging RDS and documented with step times
Celery queue drain runbook covers graceful shutdown without message loss
Salesforce re-sync runbook covers partial failure recovery without data duplication
Capacity planning model projects costs at 1K/10K/100K active contacts

plan.description: Execute end-to-end system tests on staging: load test (k6, 500 concurrent users), Salesforce migration stress test (250K contacts), email throughput test (100K sends/hour), and full pipeline-to-report smoke test.
acceptance_criteria: API p95 latency <500ms and p99 <1s under 500 concurrent users
Error rate <0.1% under full load
Salesforce import of 250K contacts completes in under 45 minutes without data loss
Email worker sustains 100K sends/hour for 30 minutes without queue backlog growth
Full journey (create contact → score → add to campaign → move deal → view report) completes in staging

plan.description: Write developer and end-user documentation: API reference (generated from OpenAPI), developer setup guide, architecture decision records (ADRs) for key choices, user guide per feature area, and Salesforce migration guide.
acceptance_criteria: API reference generated from openapi.yaml with no broken links
Developer setup guide produces running local environment from zero on clean machine
ADRs cover: database choice, email provider selection, lead scoring architecture, Salesforce sync strategy
Salesforce migration guide covers pre-migration checklist, mapping guide, and post-migration validation
All user guides include screenshots from production-equivalent UI

flaws: Elasticsearch referenced in step 12 acceptance criteria ('elasticsearch_search' feature) but never added to Docker Compose (step 11), Terraform (step 27), or any infra step. The entire search feature has no infrastructure backing it.

Step 15 depends on step 14 (email automation) but lead scoring only needs contacts (step 12) and deal activity (step 13). This creates a false sequential bottleneck that blocks scoring until the entire email engine ships.

A/B test configuration is built in the frontend (step 20) but the backend email engine (step 14) never implements A/B variant routing, split percentage enforcement, or statistical significance calculation. Feature is half-built.

Step 3 acceptance criteria states 'MVP scope is achievable in ≤12 weeks by a 3-person team.' This plan has 6 major feature areas, Salesforce CDC sync, AI lead scoring, and SOC 2 controls. That estimate is not grounded in reality — a realistic 3-person MVP is 9–12 months.

LLM-based Multi-Agent Coordinator for lead scoring (step 15) with 5 specialist agents per score event at 100K contacts nightly = 500K LLM calls per night. At any real pricing, this is cost-prohibitive and the 30-minute SLA is unachievable. This should be a rules engine or lightweight ML model, not LLM agents.

WebSocket real-time updates in step 19 require pub/sub coordination across multiple API pods. With ECS Fargate running multiple API containers, WebSocket connections are pod-local. Redis pub/sub fanout is never specified anywhere in the plan.

No dedicated authentication implementation step. JWT issuance, refresh token rotation, OAuth2 callback handling, and session management are referenced in the API spec (step 10) but no backend step owns their implementation.

PDF export with embedded charts (step 16) requires headless Chrome (Playwright/Puppeteer server-side) or a PDF generation library. Neither is in the backend stack (step 11) or infra (step 27). This is a missing service dependency.

Salesforce Change Data Capture (step 17) requires Enterprise Edition or higher. Professional and Essentials orgs do not have CDC. The migration tool will silently fail for a significant portion of the target market without a fallback polling strategy.

CSV import in step 12 sets the bar at 10K rows without timeout. A Salesforce migration tool targeting 100K+ contacts with a 10K limit is inconsistent — the Salesforce full import target is 250K contacts (step 29). The import bar should match the migration scale.

Outbound webhook delivery to customers (in the API spec, step 10) has no backend implementation step. Step 14 handles inbound SendGrid webhooks but there is no step for building the outbound webhook dispatcher, retry queue, or signature signing.

Multi-tenancy is mentioned as a row-level security requirement in step 9 but there is no step defining the tenant provisioning model, per-tenant resource limits, or how tenant context propagates through the Celery task queue.
suggestions: Replace Elasticsearch with PostgreSQL full-text search (tsvector/GIN indexes) for MVP. Defer Elasticsearch to v1 if query performance demands it — this eliminates an unbudgeted infrastructure dependency.

Rebuild lead scoring as a configurable rules engine with weighted dimensions (no LLM agents). Reserve LLM calls for score explanation generation only (on-demand, not on every recompute). This makes the 30-minute nightly batch SLA achievable.
Move step 15 dependency from [14] to [12, 13]. Email engagement is one scoring dimension — it can be added as an optional signal after the core scoring engine ships.

Add a step 11.5: Authentication Service — implement JWT issuance, refresh rotation, password hashing (bcrypt/argon2), MFA (TOTP), and OAuth2 callback handling as a first-class deliverable before any protected endpoint is built.

Add Redis pub/sub fanout to the WebSocket architecture. Document in step 19 that the PipelineBoard connects to a single WebSocket gateway that subscribes to a Redis channel broadcast by any API pod on deal mutation.
Add headless Chrome (Chromium via Playwright) as a backend sidecar service for PDF generation, or adopt a library like WeasyPrint. Define it in step 11 and the Terraform module in step 27.
Add a fallback polling sync mode to step 17 for Salesforce orgs without CDC access. Gate CDC vs. polling on the detected org edition during the OAuth connect step.

Salesforce migration rollback via 'snapshot before import' at 250K contacts will take significant time and storage. Use point-in-time recovery on RDS (already configured) plus a sync_log table as the rollback mechanism — faster and cheaper.

Step 20 A/B test: add variant routing logic to step 14 backend — store variant assignment per contact in email_sends, enforce percentage splits at send time via Celery, and add a basic significance calculator to the reports API (step 16).

SOC 2 Type II language in step 8 is misleading — you cannot complete Type II in a single planning step. Rename to 'SOC 2 Type II Readiness Framework' and add a note that certification requires a 6–12 month observation period post-launch.
missing: Elasticsearch (or OpenSearch) service in Docker Compose, Terraform, and infra — or an explicit decision to use PostgreSQL FTS instead.
SPF, DKIM, and DMARC DNS configuration for email sending domains. Without these, campaigns will land in spam regardless of CAN-SPAM compliance.
API rate limiting implementation — both per-tenant REST API throttling and per-campaign email send rate limiting (required by SendGrid and ISPs).
Data retention and deletion policy implementation — GDPR right-to-erasure requires contact hard-delete capability with cascade, not just soft-delete. No step covers this.

Application-level observability: structured logging (JSON to CloudWatch), distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry), and error tracking (Sentry or equivalent). CloudWatch alarms in step 27 cover infra but not application errors.
Tenant provisioning and onboarding flow — how does a new customer get a database schema, subdomain, and initial admin account? No step covers this.
Outbound webhook dispatcher service with retry queue, exponential backoff, dead-letter handling, and HMAC signature signing per delivery.
Security penetration test or DAST scan step — the STRIDE threat model (step 6) is design-time only. No dynamic testing of the running application is scheduled before production launch.
security_risks: Salesforce OAuth tokens stored in the database (referenced in step 6 threat model) need encryption at the application layer, not just AES-256 at rest on disk. If the database is compromised, tokens must not be recoverable in plaintext. No token-level encryption implementation is specified.

Custom report builder (step 16) accepts 'arbitrary dimension/metric/filter combinations.' The acceptance criteria notes 'without SQL injection risk' but provides no implementation guidance. A naive ORM query builder with user-supplied field names is a classic injection vector — requires an explicit allowlist of valid columns/tables.

Email open tracking pixel (step 14) serves a unique image URL per send. Without rate limiting or nonce validation on the pixel endpoint, it can be abused to probe which contact IDs exist in the system (enumeration attack).

CSV bulk import (step 12) with no mention of file size limits, MIME type validation, or malicious content scanning. A crafted CSV with formula injection (=CMD|...) targeting Excel users viewing exports is a known attack vector.

Rollback button in step 23 is disabled 'after 48h post-import' — but the rollback endpoint itself, if not protected by an additional confirmation token or admin-only RBAC, could be triggered accidentally or maliciously during the 48h window by any authenticated user.
WebSocket connections in step 19 carry deal financial data in real-time. No mention of WebSocket authentication — connections must present and validate JWT on upgrade, not just at REST API layer.
final_review.summary: This is a technically thorough plan with strong coverage of compliance, UX, testing, and operations — the dependency graph is mostly correct and the acceptance criteria are specific. However, it has several production-blocking gaps that must be fixed before implementation begins: Elasticsearch is referenced as a core feature with no infrastructure anywhere in the plan; the LLM-agent-based lead scoring architecture is economically and technically unworkable at the stated scale; there is no authentication implementation step despite auth being foundational to every other backend step; and the 12-week/3-person MVP estimate is not credible for this scope. The missing SPF/DKIM/DMARC setup is a deliverability killer for any email product. Fix the Elasticsearch decision (commit to PostgreSQL FTS or add it to the stack everywhere), replace the scoring architecture with a rules engine, add an auth step before step 12, and reframe the timeline before any implementation begins. As written, this plan would produce a partially functional system with a broken search feature, undeliverable emails, and no scalable scoring engine.

Step 15 depends on step 14 (email automation) but lead scoring only needs contacts (step 12) and deal activity (step 13). This creates a false sequential bottleneck that blocks scoring until the entire email engine ships.

A/B test configuration is built in the frontend (step 20) but the backend email engine (step 14) never implements A/B variant routing, split percentage enforcement, or statistical significance calculation. Feature is half-built.

Step 3 acceptance criteria states 'MVP scope is achievable in ≤12 weeks by a 3-person team.' This plan has 6 major feature areas, Salesforce CDC sync, AI lead scoring, and SOC 2 controls. That estimate is not grounded in reality — a realistic 3-person MVP is 9–12 months.

At any real pricing, this is cost-prohibitive and the 30-minute SLA is unachievable. This should be a rules engine or lightweight ML model, not LLM agents.

WebSocket real-time updates in step 19 require pub/sub coordination across multiple API pods. With ECS Fargate running multiple API containers, WebSocket connections are pod-local. Redis pub/sub fanout is never specified anywhere in the plan.

PDF export with embedded charts (step 16) requires headless Chrome (Playwright/Puppeteer server-side) or a PDF generation library. Neither is in the backend stack (step 11) or infra (step 27). This is a missing service dependency.

Salesforce Change Data Capture (step 17) requires Enterprise Edition or higher. Professional and Essentials orgs do not have CDC. The migration tool will silently fail for a significant portion of the target market without a fallback polling strategy.

CSV import in step 12 sets the bar at 10K rows without timeout. A Salesforce migration tool targeting 100K+ contacts with a 10K limit is inconsistent — the Salesforce full import target is 250K contacts (step 29). The import bar should match the migration scale.

Outbound webhook delivery to customers (in the API spec, step 10) has no backend implementation step. Step 14 handles inbound SendGrid webhooks but there is no step for building the outbound webhook dispatcher, retry queue, or signature signing.

Multi-tenancy is mentioned as a row-level security requirement in step 9 but there is no step defining the tenant provisioning model, per-tenant resource limits, or how tenant context propagates through the Celery task queue.

history.summary: This is a technically thorough plan with strong coverage of compliance, UX, testing, and operations — the dependency graph is mostly correct and the acceptance criteria are specific. However, it has several production-blocking gaps that must be fixed before implementation begins: Elasticsearch is referenced as a core feature with no infrastructure anywhere in the plan; the LLM-agent-based lead scoring architecture is economically and technically unworkable at the stated scale; there is no authentication implementation step despite auth being foundational to every other backend step; and the 12-week/3-person MVP estimate is not credible for this scope. The missing SPF/DKIM/DMARC setup is a deliverability killer for any email product. Fix the Elasticsearch decision (commit to PostgreSQL FTS or add it to the stack everywhere), replace the scoring architecture with a rules engine, add an auth step before step 12, and reframe the timeline before any implementation begins. As written, this plan would produce a partially functional system with a broken search feature, undeliverable emails, and no scalable scoring engine.
